mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of periods with declining FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings decline, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real sales of existing homes and housing starts signal a synchronized housing market recovery that boosts financial sector leverage and consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint upward movements in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values indicate expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that translates into immediate positive earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 5-day returns in >60% of observations within the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects strengthening labor demand that supports household income and consumption, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS changes coincide with negative 20-day forward returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 310.0, inflation expectations trigger central bank tightening fears that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 310.0 are followed by positive 20-day returns in >55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP and falling real sales of existing homes indicates a broad-based demand contraction that suppresses corporate revenues, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Concurrent declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with falling federal funds rates during monetary tightening cycles signals policy accommodation that offsets supply constraints, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling FEDFUNDS yields negative 5-day returns in >65% of observations within the tightening regime.
